title: Future Husband

jokes.text: did I put a toaster waffle into my D.V.D. player?
jokes.setup: Jack asking about bizarre behavior after oral surgery
jokes.punchline: The specific absurd action of putting food into electronic device
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd image that establishes the drug-induced confusion premise

jokes.text: You watched it for about an hour, said Nicole Kidman should get an Oscar for it, then you turned it off
jokes.setup: The waffle in DVD player
jokes.punchline: Jack not only watched it but critically evaluated the 'performance'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the absurd premise - watching a waffle 'perform' and giving critical commentary

jokes.text: You kept trying to order home massages off of craigslist
jokes.setup: More stories about Jack's drugged behavior
jokes.punchline: The implication of what 'home massages' from Craigslist really are
jokes.explanation: Solid setup for the following visual gag

jokes.text: Hey, somebody order a massage? / Oh, brother. / You're too late! I already killed her!
jokes.setup: The Craigslist massage story
jokes.punchline: Liz's dark joke about 'killing' the masseuse
jokes.explanation: Good callback with dark humor twist

jokes.text: If the pervert community gets wind of morning jogging, God help us
jokes.setup: Women who look like Liz jogging
jokes.punchline: Fear of attracting unwanted attention from 'pervert community'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with perfect Liz paranoia

jokes.text: How could a company from Philadelphia buy a company from New York? That would be like Vietnam defeating the United States in a ground war
jokes.setup: Jack dismissing the NBC buyout possibility
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to something that actually happened historically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy obliviousness - comparing NBC buyout to Vietnam War while missing the irony

jokes.text: It means the book is filled with cubes of knowledge
jokes.setup: Liz questioning the title 'Geiss Cubes'
jokes.punchline: Jack's sincere explanation of the pun
jokes.explanation: Jack earnestly explaining a terrible pun fits his character

jokes.text: Because a woman's brain has fewer folds / The Negroid musculature...
jokes.setup: Jack claiming the book has timeless principles
jokes.punchline: Reading increasingly outdated/offensive passages
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing how 'timeless' the book really is

jokes.text: On his way to work, he found some magic beans, and because he believed in them...
jokes.setup: Kenneth asked to make up excuse for Frank
jokes.punchline: Creating an elaborate fairy tale excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth absurdist sincerity

jokes.text: Just like that movie I only saw the first 10 minutes of- Fatal Attraction
jokes.setup: Kenneth calling Liz's situation romantic
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to a thriller about obsession, but only knowing the beginning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence - missing the dark turn of Fatal Attraction

jokes.text: He could wear a thumb ring
jokes.setup: Liz listing why mystery man could be bad
jokes.punchline: Equating thumb rings with being a serial killer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about fashion red flags

jokes.text: Everything always works out for the best
jokes.setup: Kenneth's optimistic motto
jokes.punchline: Sets up the wallet demonstration
jokes.explanation: Setup for the wallet throw demonstration

jokes.text: I just threw my wallet out the window
jokes.setup: Kenneth demonstrating his optimism motto
jokes.punchline: Actually throwing his wallet away to prove a point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth logic - proving optimism through self-sabotage

jokes.text: A future Tony-nominated actor
jokes.setup: Tracy being told to do another take
jokes.punchline: His outrageous self-importance about Tony eligibility
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy ego humor

jokes.text: I'm doing a one-man show. / Tonight. / What am I, a nerd? I'm going to keep it loose
jokes.setup: Tracy claiming Broadway casting
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's unprepared, same-night performance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Tracy's delusions

jokes.text: Maybe we are legend. You're Will Smith and I'm the dog
jokes.setup: Jonathan wondering if they're the last people on earth
jokes.punchline: Assigning himself the dog role from I Am Legend
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference with self-deprecating twist

jokes.text: I'll tell him it's a guy I'm having sex with
jokes.setup: How to explain unnamed executive source
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of using sex for journalism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Avery's ruthlessness

jokes.text: Rhubarb, rhubarb, golf. Prostate
jokes.setup: Jack pretending to be in a meeting
jokes.punchline: Nonsense words meant to sound like business conversation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - 'prostate' as business jargon

jokes.text: But if anything should happen to me, I want you to read, Oh, the Places You'll Go at my funeral
jokes.setup: Jonathan accepting dangerous mission
jokes.punchline: Choosing children's book for funeral reading
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonathan character detail

jokes.text: I've had this pamphlet printed up. It was expensive, but I'm really tired of discussing this with you
jokes.setup: Liz asking about eating hard cheeses
jokes.punchline: Doctor created a pamphlet specifically to avoid this conversation
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag about medical inefficiency and patient obsessions

jokes.text: He has some gum recession. Four-pockets on 14 and 15. But overall, very good teeth. Especially for an Englishman
jokes.setup: Liz asking what mystery man looks like
jokes.punchline: Doctor describing him purely in dental terms, ending with stereotype
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - dentist can only see people in dental terms

jokes.text: Well, If that's how you feel, then you're not really a patient. And people who aren't patients don't get toys from the treat bucket
jokes.setup: Liz saying he's not really a doctor
jokes.punchline: Doctor's petty retaliation about toy bucket
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of petty professional conflict

jokes.text: But there's a Batman in there! / Yeah, and if you wind him up, he swims in the bath
jokes.setup: Liz wanting the toy
jokes.punchline: Doctor describing the specific toy to torture her
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the petty conflict

jokes.text: Late at night, these two, little, twin girls told me they wanted to play with me forever
jokes.setup: Jenna describing sleeping at the office
jokes.punchline: Reference to The Shining twins, treated as real encounter
jokes.explanation: Clever horror movie reference treated as mundane experience

jokes.text: It's as if 'Claps-Giving Yay Harade' has lost all meaning
jokes.setup: Reading review about Tracy's show
jokes.punchline: Jenna's mangled version of 'clap giving parade'
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna malapropism

jokes.text: Five hours
jokes.setup: Jenna saying he babbled for an hour
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was actually five hours
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing Tracy's self-indulgence

jokes.text: I've been petitioning for them to add a category for Living Theatrically in Normal Life
jokes.setup: Jenna knowing Tony rules
jokes.punchline: Her attempt to create award for her lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna line - completely self-centered but brilliantly specific

jokes.text: Honey, I'm home! / Pac-Man, I'm Jewish! / Jeffrey, we lost the tournament!
jokes.setup: Tracy claiming he never does same thing twice
jokes.punchline: Three random, unconnected line deliveries
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist demonstration of Tracy's 'spontaneous' acting

jokes.text: Do they give an award for Tarantula Misplacement?
jokes.setup: Looking for something starting with 'T' for EGOT
jokes.punchline: Completely random and specific fake award category
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and random Tracy logic

jokes.text: It keeps me from... Hee-haw, hee-haw! Don't worry, it's just a donkey spell
jokes.setup: Kenneth mentioning a prescription
jokes.punchline: Turning into a donkey mid-sentence, treating it as normal
jokes.explanation: Peak 30 Rock absurdism - completely unexpected and perfectly Kenneth

jokes.text: Even if he does come from a country that's nothing more than the dried husk America came out of
jokes.setup: Kenneth being supportive about English mystery man
jokes.punchline: Backhanded insult to England while being 'positive'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth line - supportive and insulting simultaneously with perfect metaphor

jokes.text: Like when Lot's daughters got him drunk to repopulate the world through incest. Or when Screech went to the masquerade ball in disguise so Lisa would kiss him
jokes.setup: Kenneth saying deceit is okay for love
jokes.punchline: Comparing biblical incest story to Saved by the Bell plot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth - treating biblical and sitcom stories as equivalent moral examples

jokes.text: So here's da ting. You need to come in today so the doctor can check dem teeth, mon
jokes.setup: Kenneth doing fake accent to trick Wesley
jokes.punchline: Terrible Jamaican accent attempt
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's innocent attempt at deception

jokes.text: You're going Irish! / Cool runnings, mon. Bobsled
jokes.setup: Kenneth's bad Jamaican accent
jokes.punchline: Switching to Irish then movie references
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Kenneth's accent confusion

jokes.text: Stay away, I will bite you! Hee-haw!
jokes.setup: Kenneth having another donkey spell
jokes.punchline: Aggressive donkey behavior
jokes.explanation: Good callback with escalation to aggressive donkey

jokes.text: Because it's romantic and I'm really good at it
jokes.setup: Avery asking why he'd want to braid her hair
jokes.punchline: Jack's sincere, confident response about hair braiding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character detail - unexpected skill delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: Back to work!
jokes.setup: Jack explaining how Geiss taught him to command a room
jokes.punchline: Demonstrating with dramatic voice command
jokes.explanation: Good demonstration of Jack's corporate theatrical training

jokes.text: Do you think it was easy for me to cut ties from my old mentor, Pat O'Brien, after he shaved his moustache?
jokes.setup: Avery explaining cutting ties from mentors
jokes.punchline: Trivial reason for abandoning professional relationship
jokes.explanation: Good Avery ruthlessness over completely superficial detail

jokes.text: Do you think it was easy for me to cut ties from my old mentor, Pat O'Brien, after he shaved his moustache?
jokes.setup: Avery explaining mentor relationships
jokes.punchline: The trivial reason for ending a mentorship
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic about professional relationships based on facial hair choices

jokes.text: Which is the song that I sang to Don at his promotion dinner
jokes.setup: Jack saying he'll 'stand by his man'
jokes.punchline: Revealing he literally sang this song to Geiss
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character detail - overly intimate business relationship

jokes.text: If I wanted to see a black guy make a fool of himself, I'd have sex with K-Fed again
jokes.setup: Being asked to support Tracy's show
jokes.punchline: Racist and personal insult via celebrity reference
jokes.explanation: Jenna being deliberately offensive in character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: And without my wallet, I don't even have my lucky rabbit's spine
jokes.setup: Kenneth worrying about what will happen
jokes.punchline: Dark twist on 'lucky rabbit's foot'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth dark innocence - making rabbit's foot more disturbing

jokes.text: Why, with a curtain five hours from now, it would take the greatest acting coach the world has ever seen to make his show a success. Fine, I'll do it!
jokes.setup: Jenna describing impossible task
jokes.punchline: Immediately volunteering herself as the solution
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna ego setup and payoff

jokes.text: Bobsled.
jokes.setup: Kenneth at dentist office, still in character
jokes.punchline: Random Jamaican reference word
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that maintains character consistency

jokes.text: Can you tell me why you come up on my phone as 'Future Wife'?
jokes.setup: Wesley calling Liz back
jokes.punchline: Revealing he also has her programmed oddly in his phone
jokes.explanation: Good symmetrical callback revealing mutual drugged contact creation

jokes.text: Wow, this is not interesting
jokes.setup: Wesley's detailed story about his dental problem
jokes.punchline: Liz's blunt assessment mid-conversation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Liz's social bluntness

jokes.text: Well, British people have notoriously bad teeth, so... / I've never heard that
jokes.setup: Liz making assumption about Wesley's teeth
jokes.punchline: Wesley genuinely not knowing this stereotype
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe humor about cultural stereotypes

jokes.text: Like when they say older women have breadback. / What's breadback? / The loaf of back fat between a woman's bra and her giant underwear
jokes.setup: Liz claiming 'breadback' is a thing people say
jokes.punchline: Her detailed explanation of completely made-up term
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Liz moment - inventing crude terms and defending them as common knowledge

jokes.text: You use that word too much
jokes.setup: Wesley saying 'situation' multiple times
jokes.punchline: Liz calling out his verbal tic
jokes.explanation: Minor observational humor about annoying speech patterns

jokes.text: Hiding him in your freezer. Just like we did with Hiram Sheinhardt during the R.C.A. deal
jokes.setup: Keeping Geiss's death secret
jokes.punchline: Revealing this is standard corporate practice
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing corporate callousness as routine

jokes.text: And if you need to pass some eye water, I'll be happy to go out and get you some weakness tissues
jokes.setup: Acknowledging Jack might cry
jokes.punchline: Corporate euphemisms for tears and tissues
jokes.explanation: Perfect corporate speak dehumanization of basic emotions

jokes.text: We were so awkward that the waitress gave us separate checks without asking. And a priest came over and asked us who we'd lost
jokes.setup: Describing disaster date with Wesley
jokes.punchline: Two escalating examples of how bad it was
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing how spectacularly bad the date was

jokes.text: Sometimes, everything is just the worst, Kenneth
jokes.setup: Kenneth's 'everything works out' philosophy being tested
jokes.punchline: Liz's blunt rejection of optimism
jokes.explanation: Good callback undermining Kenneth's earlier optimism

jokes.text: They're selling N.B.C. to a company called KableTown. With a 'K'
jokes.setup: Jack announcing the sale
jokes.punchline: The horror of the 'K' spelling
jokes.explanation: Good Jack horror at corporate tackiness

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, my parents have KableTown down in Pennsylvania. It's a fine and generous company
jokes.setup: Jack's horror at KableTown
jokes.punchline: Liz's casual endorsement
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Jack's drama

jokes.text: That's the sound of me being erased from contact lists all around the world
jokes.setup: Jack claiming to hear something
jokes.punchline: Paranoid interpretation of silence
jokes.explanation: Good Jack paranoia and corporate anxiety

jokes.text: It's the Hug Plane coming in for a landing? You're cleared for approach
jokes.setup: Liz offering comfort to Jack
jokes.punchline: Aviation metaphor for hugging
jokes.explanation: Decent Liz awkwardness with physical affection

jokes.text: Got it, no farting
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining acting is about consistency and control
jokes.punchline: Tracy's interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy misunderstanding of acting advice

jokes.text: After me. / No, Tracy. / No, stop it, not this part. / No, stop it. / Up. / We've got to start over. / I farted
jokes.setup: Acting lesson with tongue twisters
jokes.punchline: Complete breakdown ending with fart
jokes.explanation: Predictable escalation with crude payoff

jokes.text: Well Guards. Guard your well... well
jokes.setup: Jenna describing her first acting job
jokes.punchline: Terrible commercial tagline
jokes.explanation: Weak pun but fits Jenna's terrible early career

jokes.text: You were a fat baby
jokes.setup: Jenna sharing vulnerable career memory
jokes.punchline: Tracy's blunt, hurtful observation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Tracy's lack of filter

jokes.text: Go out there and be yourself. Go on stage and read the damn phone book, for all I care!
jokes.setup: Jenna giving up on coaching Tracy
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic suggestion that becomes literal advice
jokes.explanation: Sets up what Tracy actually does in his show

jokes.text: No, this is not possible. / But it is. / I knew my wallet would turn up. And it did
jokes.setup: Wesley found Kenneth's wallet
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's vindication of his optimism philosophy
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Kenneth's optimism theme

jokes.text: Oh, I am either very happy right now, or I'm having a pretty bad donkey spell
jokes.setup: Kenneth realizing the coincidence
jokes.punchline: Uncertainty about his emotional state vs. donkey transformation
jokes.explanation: Good callback with perfect Kenneth confusion about his own emotional state

jokes.text: Do you like Tex-Mex? / No, I don't. / See you there
jokes.setup: Making dinner plans
jokes.punchline: Going somewhere she doesn't want to go
jokes.explanation: Good relationship comedy about compromise

jokes.text: According to my sources, the clear frontrunner current G.E. executive, Jack Donaghy... with the programming experience, business savvy, and piercing, blue eyes of a Siberian husky
jokes.setup: News report about new NBC head
jokes.punchline: Personal description slipping into news report
jokes.explanation: Good Avery mixing personal and professional

jokes.text: I'm going to celebrate by buying my lady friend that necklace she pointed out and then, cooking her a Western omelette
jokes.setup: Avery quoting Jack's supposed statement
jokes.punchline: Mundane domestic plans presented as business quote
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between public and private Jack

jokes.text: And then, I'm going to braid your hair
jokes.setup: Jack watching the news report
jokes.punchline: Adding the detail Avery left out
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier hair braiding discussion

jokes.text: [Tracy reading phone book names on stage] Klarsfeld, Rubin M. Klarvet, Yuri...
jokes.setup: Tracy performing his one-man show
jokes.punchline: Literally reading the phone book as Jenna suggested
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Tracy took Jenna's sarcasm literally

jokes.text: Bravo! It's a triumph!
jokes.setup: Tracy reading phone book on stage
jokes.punchline: Audience loving this terrible performance
jokes.explanation: Good ironic ending showing Tracy's inexplicable success

tmdb_overview: Liz searches for a man she met in her dentist's waiting room. Jack fears for the future when he learns a cable company may have its sights on NBC. Jenna helps Tracy try to get a Tony award.
insight: High-density absurdist hour delivers 70 jokes at 3 per minute, anchored by character-driven callbacks.
